articles.title: Bitcoin Up or Down - April 8, 4:35AM-4:40AM ET
articles.summary: Bitcoin faces a critical 5-minute window on April 8th between 4:35AM and 4:40AM ET that could determine its next major price direction. This ultra-specific timeframe suggests a scheduled event or announcement that savvy crypto bettors are already positioning for, making it prime territory for short-term prediction markets.

articles.title: Trump announces Hormuz deadline extension today? Predicti...
articles.summary: Trump appears set to announce an extension of some deadline related to the Strait of Hormuz, the critical oil shipping chokepoint between Iran and the Arabian Peninsula. Geopolitical tension markets will move fast on this news, especially oil futures and Middle East conflict probabilities.

articles.title: Democratic lawmakers demand oversight on prediction markets and crackdown on wagers on war
articles.summary: Democratic lawmakers are pushing for increased regulation of prediction markets and want to ban betting on military conflicts and war outcomes. This signals growing political pressure that could reshape which events traders can bet on and how platforms operate in the US.

articles.title: Polymarket Rebels Still Betting in Banned Countries Using VPNs
articles.summary: Polymarket users in restricted countries are circumventing geographic bans by using VPNs to place bets on the platform. This underground activity shows prediction markets have enough appeal to drive users toward technical workarounds, potentially forcing regulators to either crack down harder or reconsider blanket prohibitions.

articles.title: The big state gamble on prediction markets
articles.summary: States are placing massive bets on prediction markets as a revenue source, regulatory framework, or both. If they get it right, we're looking at legitimized mainstream adoption, but if they botch the regulations, it could set the industry back years.

articles.title: House Democrats call on federal regulator to crack down on offshore prediction market war bets
articles.summary: House Democrats are pushing federal regulators to ban or restrict prediction markets that allow betting on wars and military conflicts, likely targeting platforms like Kalshi that operate offshore betting exchanges. The crackdown could signal broader regulatory hostility toward prediction markets, especially those covering sensitive geopolitical events that lawmakers view as inappropriate for wagering.

articles.title: K-Pop Prediction Hunters | Opinion
articles.summary: K-Pop fans are apparently using their obsessive data-tracking skills to hunt down and exploit prediction market opportunities around Korean pop culture events. These stans already master complex voting algorithms and social media manipulation, so they're naturally equipped to spot mispriced odds on album sales, chart positions, and award show outcomes before the books catch up.

articles.title: Carson City judge extends statewide ban for Kalshi
articles.summary: A Carson City judge has extended Nevada's statewide ban preventing Kalshi from operating within state borders. This keeps one of the biggest prediction market platforms locked out of a key gambling-friendly jurisdiction, limiting their growth potential and giving competitors like PredictIt and Polymarket more runway in the space.

articles.title: Polymarket Readies Platform Overhaul With New Collateral Token
articles.summary: Polymarket is overhauling its platform with a new collateral token, moving away from its current USDC-only system. This could slash transaction fees and attract more international users who've been priced out by Ethereum gas costs and stablecoin restrictions.

articles.title: Polymarket reveals a 'full exchange upgrade' to take control of its own trading and truth
articles.summary: Polymarket is launching a major platform overhaul that will give them direct control over both trade execution and outcome resolution instead of relying on third-party infrastructure. This shift could eliminate the friction and delays that have plagued the platform, making it faster and more reliable for high-stakes political betting when every minute counts.

articles.title: New Jersey cannot regulate Kalshi's prediction market, US appeals court rules
articles.summary: A federal appeals court ruled that New Jersey lacks authority to regulate Kalshi's prediction markets, blocking the state's regulatory interference. The decision strengthens prediction markets' legal standing and signals that federal oversight trumps state-level attempts to shut down legitimate betting platforms.

articles.title: Polymarket apologizes after letting users bet on downed U.S. pilots in Iran: 'It should not have been posted'
articles.summary: Polymarket allowed users to place bets on whether U.S. pilots would be shot down in Iranian airspace before quickly removing the market and issuing an apology. The incident highlights how prediction markets can cross ethical lines when real human lives become betting fodder, potentially inviting regulatory scrutiny that could threaten the industry's growth.
